description:
A. Background  
     You are assisting an “artifact-manager” who curates binary repositories that live under  
     /home/user/repositories.  The manager wants a tiny audit toolkit that relies **only** on the
     classic “find  … | xargs …” work-flow (no loops, no “while read”, no GNU-parallel, etc.).
     The audit results must be written in a very strict, machine-verifiable format, so please
     follow every detail below.

  B. Directories that already exist  
     /home/user/repositories  
       └── repoA  
           ├── oldlib-1.0.jar                 (older than 90 days)  
           ├── newlib-2.0.jar                 (recent)  
           ├── package-1.0.tar.gz             (recent)  
           ├── empty.tmp                      (size 0)  
           └── nested/  
               ├── legacy-0.9.jar             (older than 90 days)  
               ├── sample-archive.tar.gz      (recent)  
               └── zero.txt                   (size 0)  
       └── repoB  
           ├── obsolete-2.3.jar               (older than 90 days)  
           ├── data-1.1.tar.gz               (recent)  
           └── nothing.txt                    (size 0)

  C. What you must create  
     1.  Directory  /home/user/artifact_audit  
         └── old-jars/           (will receive copies of aged *.jar files)

     2.  File      /home/user/artifact_audit/tarball_sha256.txt  
         • One line per “fresh” *.tar.gz file ( mtime < 30 days ).  
         • Each line **exactly** in this pipe-separated format  
              relative/path/from/repositories|<size_in_bytes>|<sha256sum>  
           – Example of the required style (do **NOT** hard-code these values):  
              repoX/demo.tar.gz|12345|2c26b46b68ffc68ff99b453c1d30413413422d706483bfa0f98a5e886266e7ae  
           – No extra spaces; one trailing newline at the end of the file.  
           – “relative/path…” must not start with “/”.

     3.  File      /home/user/artifact_audit/removed_zero_byte_files.log  
         • One absolute path per line, listing every file actually deleted  
           because it was zero bytes.  Example style:  
              /home/user/repositories/repoA/empty.tmp  
         • Lines may appear in any order but must cover every removed zero-byte file.  
         • End the file with a single newline (no blank lines after the list).

  D. Operations (must all be done with find  … | xargs chains):  
     1. Copy every “aged” *.jar ( mtime > 90 days ) from **any repo** into  
        /home/user/artifact_audit/old-jars/  
        • Use a simple flat layout: the copied files all land directly inside
          old-jars/ (no sub-directories).  
        • Do **NOT** delete the originals.  

     2. Produce tarball_sha256.txt for every *.tar.gz newer than 30 days.  
        • Compute sha256sum of the file content (same value you would get from
          “sha256sum” without the filename).  
        • Capture the file size in bytes (same value you would get from “stat -c%s”).  
        • Generate the line in the exact order  
          “relativePath|size|hash” (see §C-2).  

     3. Delete every zero-byte file anywhere under /home/user/repositories and
        record what you deleted in removed_zero_byte_files.log.  

  E. Constraints  
     • All three steps must be executed with at most 3–4 “find … | xargs …” style
       command lines in total.  
     • Do not rely on Bash loops, ‘while read’, or external scripting languages.  
     • Do not run anything as root; you have normal user permissions only.  
     • Do not remove or modify any file other than the requested zero-byte ones.  
     • All paths in the log files must follow the exact specifications above—
       automated tests will check them verbatim.

  F. How the result will be tested  
     1. The tester will verify that  /home/user/artifact_audit/old-jars/  contains
        exactly the *.jar files whose mtime is older than 90 days and that the
        copied files’ contents match their originals byte-for-byte.  
     2. The tester will independently compute size and sha256 of every “fresh”
        *.tar.gz and compare them with the lines in  tarball_sha256.txt.  
     3. The tester will confirm that every zero-byte file was deleted and that
        its absolute path appears once—and only once—in
        removed_zero_byte_files.log.  
     4. Both log files must end with a single trailing newline and have no
        extraneous blank lines.

  G. Deliverable  
     After you finish, the following three filesystem objects must exist and be
     correct:  
        /home/user/artifact_audit/old-jars/…  
        /home/user/artifact_audit/tarball_sha256.txt  
        /home/user/artifact_audit/removed_zero_byte_files.log  
     No console or textual output beyond these files is required, although you
     may print helpful progress messages while running commands.

  Please carry out the task now, strictly following the constraints above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data used by the grader.
    Everything below this line is invisible to the agent.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <dir path="/home/user/repositories">
      <dir path="repoA">
        <file name="oldlib-1.0.jar"     size="31"  mtime="2020-01-01 00:00:00"
              contents="OLDLIB 1.0 binary placeholder\n"/>
        <file name="newlib-2.0.jar"     size="31"  mtime="NOW"
              contents="NEWLIB 2.0 binary placeholder\n"/>
        <file name="package-1.0.tar.gz" size="38"  mtime="NOW"
              contents="This is dummy tarball for package 1.0\n"/>
        <file name="empty.tmp"          size="0"   mtime="NOW"
              contents=""/>
        <dir path="nested">
          <file name="legacy-0.9.jar"      size="18" mtime="2020-02-01 00:00:00"
                contents="LEGACY 0.9 binary\n"/>
          <file name="sample-archive.tar.gz" size="36" mtime="NOW-10d"
                contents="Sample archive inside nested folder\n"/>
          <file name="zero.txt"             size="0"  mtime="NOW"
                contents=""/>
        </dir>
      </dir>
      <dir path="repoB">
        <file name="obsolete-2.3.jar"  size="28" mtime="2019-12-01 00:00:00"
              contents="OBSOLETE 2.3 binary archive\n"/>
        <file name="data-1.1.tar.gz"   size="27" mtime="NOW-20d"
              contents="Data archive 1.1 for repoB\n"/>
        <file name="nothing.txt"       size="0"  mtime="NOW"
              contents=""/>
      </dir>
    </dir>
  </initial_state>

  <expected_final_state>
    <dir path="/home/user/artifact_audit">
      <dir path="old-jars">
        <!-- copies of all *.jar with mtime > 90 days -->
        <file name="oldlib-1.0.jar"     size="31"/>
        <file name="legacy-0.9.jar"     size="18"/>
        <file name="obsolete-2.3.jar"   size="28"/>
      </dir>

      <file name="tarball_sha256.txt">
        <!--
          Must contain exactly three lines, one per tarball newer than 30 days,
          with the correct size and SHA-256 calculated at grading time:
            repoA/package-1.0.tar.gz|38|<sha256>
            repoA/nested/sample-archive.tar.gz|36|<sha256>
            repoB/data-1.1.tar.gz|27|<sha256>
        -->
      </file>

      <file name="removed_zero_byte_files.log">
        <!-- Must contain the three absolute paths below, one per line -->
        /home/user/repositories/repoA/empty.tmp
        /home/user/repositories/repoA/nested/zero.txt
        /home/user/repositories/repoB/nothing.txt
      </file>
    </dir>

    <!-- zero-byte files must no longer be present -->
    <deleted path="/home/user/repositories/repoA/empty.tmp"/>
    <deleted path="/home/user/repositories/repoA/nested/zero.txt"/>
    <deleted path="/home/user/repositories/repoB/nothing.txt"/>
  </expected_final_state>
